jokes.text: Marge, can't we get some clear plates? I can't see the TV!
jokes.setup: Family dinner setting where Homer should be focused on family
jokes.punchline: Homer's complaint reveals his priority is watching TV through his plate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - his selfishness and TV obsession condensed into one absurd complaint

jokes.text: If you really wanted us to be neater, you'd serve us out of one long bowl. / You're talking about a trough. We're not going to eat from a trough.
jokes.setup: Homer suggests a practical solution to messy eating
jokes.punchline: Marge points out he's describing how animals eat, Homer doesn't see the problem
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from reasonable suggestion to animal behavior, with perfect character reactions

jokes.text: Why are you in your underwear? / Hey, this ain't the Ritz.
jokes.setup: Marge questions Homer's inappropriate dinner attire
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal using hotel comparison
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment but predictable Homer response

jokes.text: Hello, Marge. How's the family? I don't want to talk about it! Mind your own business.
jokes.setup: Marge tries to have a civilized conversation alone
jokes.punchline: She immediately contradicts herself by being hostile to her own pleasantries
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of Marge's attempt at normalcy

jokes.text: Yeah, pretty much, except we drove around in a van solving mysteries.
jokes.setup: Marge asks if this chaos is what Homer pictured for married life
jokes.punchline: Homer's only complaint is the lack of Scooby-Doo adventure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - Homer's satisfied with chaos, just wanted cartoon adventure elements

jokes.text: Oh, I hate having parties. The toilet always gets backed up.
jokes.setup: Marge wants to throw a dinner party
jokes.punchline: Homer's concern is purely practical and gross
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer bathroom humor, functional but not special

jokes.text: I don't care if the sink shoots sludge. We're having a party.
jokes.setup: Homer complains about party problems
jokes.punchline: Marge's desperation expressed through extreme imagery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Marge's determination

jokes.text: Man, that is flagrant false advertising!
jokes.setup: Bart looking at something in kitchen store
jokes.punchline: Bart's sophisticated vocabulary about consumer fraud
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Bart using adult terminology

jokes.text: Oh, we can't afford that. Who do you think I am, Liz Taylor? / Well, maybe we could use it once and then return it.
jokes.setup: Marge claims they can't afford expensive punch bowl
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggests fraudulent return scheme
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Marge's moral flexibility when it comes to entertaining

jokes.text: So I helped myself to a few of the better umbrellas.
jokes.setup: Reverend mentions his coat was stolen at interfaith banquet
jokes.punchline: Religious leader casually admits to theft in retaliation
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of religious authority figure expectations

jokes.text: Well, as you can see, we don't believe fur is murder... but paying for it sure is.
jokes.setup: Helen commenting on her fur coat
jokes.punchline: Twist on animal rights slogan becomes about frugality
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on familiar slogan

jokes.text: She doesn't want anyone to know she's got no eyebrows. What? You don't.
jokes.setup: Kirk explaining why they're late
jokes.punchline: Kirk immediately reveals the embarrassing secret his wife didn't want shared
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Kirk's obliviousness and marriage problems

jokes.text: You know, I like his films, except for that nervous fella's always in 'em.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Woody Allen movies
jokes.punchline: Speaker doesn't realize Woody Allen is the nervous guy in his own films
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about casual cultural ignorance

jokes.text: Should have asked them to hurl some bacon. Then maybe I could have had a decent breakfast for once.
jokes.setup: Kirk mentions teenagers egged their car
jokes.punchline: Kirk wishes for breakfast food vandalism instead
jokes.explanation: Dark humor revealing Kirk's domestic dissatisfaction

jokes.text: You know what you two need? A little comic strip called Love Is. It's about two naked eight-year-olds who are married.
jokes.setup: Homer offers marriage advice to fighting couple
jokes.punchline: Homer's helpful suggestion is deeply inappropriate comic strip
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - well-meaning but completely inappropriate suggestion

jokes.text: I'd tell the warden, 'Bring on the lethal injection!'
jokes.setup: Ned complimenting Marge's cooking using last meal metaphor
jokes.punchline: Wholesome Ned eagerly embracing death penalty imagery
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of Ned's character through dark metaphor

jokes.text: Oh, that's a noodle-scratcher.
jokes.setup: Someone thinking about charades
jokes.punchline: Malapropism for 'head-scratcher' using dinner context
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay, mildly amusing

jokes.text: It's good for keeping down the urges.
jokes.setup: Discussion about cornstarch
jokes.punchline: Dr. Hibbert suggests medical use for cooking ingredient
jokes.explanation: Absurd medical advice from Dr. Hibbert

jokes.text: Do you want me to show this to the cat and have the cat tell you what it is? 'Cause the cat's gonna get it.
jokes.setup: Kirk frustrated with Luann not guessing charades
jokes.punchline: Kirk's condescending suggestion that a cat would understand better
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Kirk's condescension and marriage toxicity

jokes.text: We didn't all go to Gudger College.
jokes.setup: Kirk acting superior about charades intelligence
jokes.punchline: Luann sarcastically references fake/terrible college
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback with made-up college name

jokes.text: Well, it's no wonder I can't draw dignity. I gave it up when I married her.
jokes.setup: Kirk struggling to draw 'dignity' in charades
jokes.punchline: Kirk blames his lack of dignity on his marriage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where the charades answer becomes perfect metaphor for Kirk's situation

jokes.text: Oh, cram it, Churchie!
jokes.setup: Reverend Lovejoy tries to calm Kirk down
jokes.punchline: Kirk rudely dismisses religious authority with childish nickname
jokes.explanation: Shows Kirk's deteriorating social filter

jokes.text: Or how about the one about how I carry a change purse? Yeah, a purse!
jokes.setup: Kirk listing his embarrassments
jokes.punchline: Kirk's emphasis on admitting to carrying purse reveals his insecurity
jokes.explanation: Good character detail revealing Kirk's masculine insecurities

jokes.text: sent the number one cracker factory in town into a tie for sixth with Table Time and Allied Biscuit.
jokes.setup: Luann detailing Kirk's business failures
jokes.punchline: Specific ranking shows dramatic fall from first to tied for sixth
jokes.explanation: Funny specificity of business failure with company names

jokes.text: I love having to steal clothes from the church donation box.
jokes.setup: Luann sarcastically listing things she loves about their poverty
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about stealing from church charity
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about poverty with religious irony

jokes.text: I want a divorce!
jokes.setup: Escalating marital argument at dinner party
jokes.punchline: Luann drops the bombshell in front of everyone
jokes.explanation: Perfect climax to the building tension - maximum awkwardness

jokes.text: It's a door! Use it!
jokes.setup: Kirk dramatically gestures at his charades drawing
jokes.punchline: Kirk's angry revelation that his abstract art was just a door
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the charades sequence with visual comedy

jokes.text: Hello, I'm Dr Hibbert. I'm afraid I'm going to have to amputate your butt.
jokes.setup: Awkward silence after divorce announcement
jokes.punchline: Bart's inappropriate Dr. Hibbert impression to break tension
jokes.explanation: Bart's attempt at comic relief, mildly funny but not exceptional

jokes.text: Oh, okay. Ta-ta! I'm off to the beauty salon!
jokes.setup: Luann just declared divorce and is leaving
jokes.punchline: Her casual, upbeat attitude about going to salon after destroying marriage
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Luann's liberation

jokes.text: Well, I think that went pretty well.
jokes.setup: Catastrophic dinner party ending in divorce
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely oblivious assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness to social disaster

jokes.text: I shouldn't have served those North Korean fortune cookies. They were so insulting- 'You are a coward.'
jokes.setup: Marge blaming herself for party disaster
jokes.punchline: Specific example of hostile fortune cookie message
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd detail about Communist fortune cookies

jokes.text: You can't keep blaming yourself. Just blame yourself once and move on.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to comfort Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer's advice is technically still self-blame
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - sounds helpful but completely misses the point

jokes.text: Aren't you gonna say 'Ha-ha'?
jokes.setup: Nelson hears about Milhouse's parents' divorce
jokes.punchline: Questions why he isn't delivering his signature cruel laugh
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-moment where character questions his own catchphrase. Shows unexpected depth and self-awareness.

jokes.text: By the end, her breath was so fresh... she wasn't really my mother anymore.
jokes.setup: Kid explaining his parents' divorce over mother's cough drop addiction
jokes.punchline: Absurd reasoning that fresh breath destroyed maternal bond
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on addiction and family dissolution

jokes.text: I sleep in a drawer.
jokes.setup: Child says he got over his parents' divorce
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of sleeping in furniture
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of disturbing living situation

jokes.text: Forget it! She's gone! Presto change-o! Kaboom! Sweet Fanny Adams! Bye-bye!
jokes.setup: Luann talking about forgetting her old self
jokes.punchline: Luann's over-the-top magical transformation language
jokes.explanation: Shows Luann's desperate reinvention but somewhat forced

jokes.text: I sleep in a racing car. Do you? / I sleep in a big bed with my wife.
jokes.setup: Kirk boasting about his cool racing car bed
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact response about normal adult arrangement
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons moment - perfect setup/punchline structure revealing Kirk's pathetic situation

jokes.text: Uh, I sleep in a racing car. Do you? I sleep in a big bed with my wife.
jokes.setup: Kirk brags about sleeping in racing car bed
jokes.punchline: Homer casually destroys him with adult relationship reality
jokes.explanation: Legendary Simpsons moment. Perfect structure, devastating comeback, maximum quotability. Homer's oblivious cruelty is comedy perfection.

jokes.text: Ah, just throw it over the fence. Let Arby's worry about it.
jokes.setup: Question about disposing of dead possum
jokes.punchline: Kirk's casual suggestion to make it Arby's problem
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kirk's irresponsible attitude

jokes.text: Kirk, crackers are a family food. Happy families. Maybe single people eat crackers. We don't know. Frankly, we don't want to know. It's a market we can do without.
jokes.setup: Kirk being fired from cracker factory
jokes.punchline: Absurd reasoning that single people are unsuitable for cracker marketing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist corporate logic - one of the episode's best jokes

jokes.text: I don't recall saying 'good luck.'
jokes.setup: Kirk mentions boss said 'so long, good luck'
jokes.punchline: Boss coldly corrects that he didn't wish Kirk luck
jokes.explanation: Perfect corporate coldness escalation

jokes.text: Hey, I know you from American Gladiators. You're Pyro!
jokes.setup: Meeting mom's new boyfriend Chase
jokes.punchline: Milhouse recognizes him from TV show
jokes.explanation: Simple recognition gag, functional but not special

jokes.text: Only on weekends. During the week, I'm just your average workaday stuntman.
jokes.setup: Chase explains his American Gladiators role
jokes.punchline: Treats dangerous stunt work as mundane day job
jokes.explanation: Understatement humor - treating stunt work as ordinary employment. Sets up his casual attitude toward danger.

jokes.text: Gee! Sorry! It's a pretty standard stunt, Homer.
jokes.setup: Homer screaming in pain from chair broken on him
jokes.punchline: Chase's casual dismissal of Homer's obvious injury
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Homer's pain and Chase's professional indifference

jokes.text: Gee! Sorry! It's a pretty standard stunt, Homer.
jokes.setup: Homer gets hurt when Chase demonstrates stunt
jokes.punchline: Chase treats Homer's injury as amateur mistake
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy setup with professional casually dismissing amateur's pain. Functional slapstick but not exceptional.

jokes.text: That doesn't sound like something I'd say.
jokes.setup: Marge says Homer agreed to go see Spalding Gray
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest assessment of his character
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware Homer moment

jokes.text: Can I have the keys to the car, lover? I feel like changing wigs.
jokes.setup: Starla asking Kirk for car keys
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre reason for needing the car
jokes.explanation: Shows Starla's flakiness and Kirk's poor rebound choice

jokes.text: Can I Borrow a Feeling?
jokes.setup: Homer reading Kirk's demo tape title
jokes.punchline: The pathetic, desperate song title
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Kirk's desperation in song title form

jokes.text: One day your wife is making you your favorite meal, the next day, you're thawing a hot dog in a gas station sink.
jokes.setup: Kirk reflects on how quickly marriage ended
jokes.punchline: Specific image of desperate bachelor food preparation
jokes.explanation: Poetic and specific image of divorced man's desperation. Great contrast between domestic comfort and pathetic reality.

jokes.text: the next day, you're thawing a hot dog in a gas station sink.
jokes.setup: Kirk describing how marriage can end suddenly
jokes.punchline: Specific, pathetic detail of bachelor existence
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and pathetic image of divorced life

jokes.text: a strong marriage built on a solid foundation of routine.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why his marriage is secure
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks routine (not love) is the foundation
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup for Homer's immediate comeuppance

jokes.text: They're thawing in the sink.
jokes.setup: Homer confident about his marriage routine
jokes.punchline: Immediate callback to Kirk's pathetic bachelor existence
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure - sets up and immediately pays off Kirk's prediction

jokes.text: You mean, like your poker shack out in the swamp?
jokes.setup: Homer worries about secrets Marge doesn't know
jokes.punchline: Lisa casually reveals Marge knows about hidden gambling location
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of Homer's paranoia - his 'secret' is already known. Lisa's casual delivery adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Levis. Hey, you think that truck is full of jeans?
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge having romantic wedding reception
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete lack of romantic focus
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer distractibility during romantic moment

jokes.text: Levis. Hey, you think that truck is full of jeans?
jokes.setup: Honeymoon scene with Levis truck passing
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple-minded literal interpretation of brand name
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer literalism - seeing brand name and assuming literal cargo. Character-appropriate but simple humor.

jokes.text: Arr, Matey!
jokes.setup: Homer making ocean sounds for Marge to sleep
jokes.punchline: Homer inexplicably becomes pirate in his ocean impression
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer escalation beyond the logical

jokes.text: Homer, what are you doing? I wanted to surprise you with a kicky summer do.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently cutting Marge's hair
jokes.punchline: Homer's misguided romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Homer's attempt at romance goes horribly wrong.

jokes.text: None! They'd have more sense than that!
jokes.setup: Homer asks how many husbands would surprise wives with haircuts
jokes.punchline: Marge's emphatic response that no sensible husband would
jokes.explanation: Good Marge exasperation moment

jokes.text: These things happen. Eight dollars.
jokes.setup: Homer files for divorce
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic casualness about marriage dissolution plus cheap fee
jokes.explanation: Perfect bureaucratic indifference to major life event. The low price adds insult to injury about marriage value.

jokes.text: I didn't want a hokey second wedding like those ones on TV. This one is for real. I got us a divorce this afternoon.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why they need second wedding
jokes.punchline: Reveals he divorced Marge to make it more authentic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - well-intentioned but completely backwards approach

jokes.text: I didn't want a hokey second wedding like those ones on TV. This one is for real. I got us a divorce this afternoon.
jokes.setup: Homer explains surprise wedding to Marge
jokes.punchline: His romantic gesture involved actual divorce for authenticity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - makes gesture more meaningful by making it legally necessary. Original concept with perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Wait! I want one last chance to enjoy single life. [Belches] Okay, ready.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to enjoy single life before remarrying
jokes.punchline: His idea of single life pleasure is just belching
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his concept of freedom is hilariously limited

jokes.text: in impotence and in potence
jokes.setup: Reading Homer's custom wedding vows
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate variation on 'sickness and health'
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer sexual humor in inappropriate context

jokes.text: 'Do you, Marge, take Homer in richness and in poorness-' 'Poorness' is underlined
jokes.setup: Minister reads Homer's custom wedding vows
jokes.punchline: Homer emphasized the poverty aspect of traditional vows
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Homer's self-awareness about financial prospects. Shows his honesty about limitations.

jokes.text: jet-powered, monkey navigated-
jokes.setup: Reading Homer's increasingly bizarre vows
jokes.punchline: Homer's vows devolve into science fiction nonsense
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's inability to write serious vows

jokes.text: Okay, everybody, who likes The Doobie Brothers? 'Cause we got one of them.
jokes.setup: Wedding reception needs entertainment
jokes.punchline: Band only has one member from famous group
jokes.explanation: Good joke about knockoff wedding entertainment

jokes.text: How about it, Luann? Will you marry me... again? - Ooh, no!
jokes.setup: Kirk's public proposal after his terrible song performance
jokes.punchline: Immediate, emphatic rejection
jokes.explanation: Predictable but satisfying rejection of Kirk's pathetic gesture. Good economy and appropriate character conclusion.

jokes.text: Ooh, no! / Oh. Well, uh, can I have my shirts back at least?
jokes.setup: Kirk's pathetic marriage proposal gets rejected
jokes.punchline: Kirk immediately asks for practical domestic items
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kirk moment - transitions from romantic to mundane instantly
